message:
Temporarily disable Redis transaction/pubsub methods and stub out x509 SPKI pinning

src/messaging/redis.rs:
- Comment out `RedisClient::transaction()` method body which starts a
  MULTI/EXEC transaction via `Transaction::begin()`. The doc comment and
  signature are preserved inside the block comment so the API surface
  remains documented but the method is not compiled, likely to avoid
  build errors or dependency issues with the Transaction type.
- Comment out `RedisClient::pubsub()` method body which opens a dedicated
  Pub/Sub connection via `RedisPubSub::connect()`. Same rationale — the
  method is temporarily disabled while preserving its documentation.

src/tls/types.rs:
- In `CertificatePin::compute_spki_sha256()`, comment out the full
  x509_parser-based implementation that parsed DER certificates,
  extracted the SPKI (Subject Public Key Info) raw bytes, and computed
  a SHA-256 digest to produce an SpkiSha256 pin.
- Replace with a stub that returns `Err(TlsError::Certificate("Not
  implemented"))`, disabling certificate pinning verification at runtime.
  The `ring::digest` import is retained but the `x509_parser` import is
  commented out, suggesting the x509_parser crate dependency may be
  removed or is causing compilation issues.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
